title: Logical Operators
description: Combine multiple conditions together with AND / OR.

slides.title: Complex Decisions 🧩
slides.content:
## Thinking in Logic

Sometimes an action requires two things to be true AT THE SAME TIME. 
*"If it is raining AND I have an umbrella, go outside."*

Other times, only one needs to be true. 
*"If I have a bike OR a car, I can get to work."*

We build these using Logical Operators.

slides.title: The AND Operator (&&) 🤝
slides.content: The `&&` symbol means AND. Both the left side AND the right side must be true for the whole statement to be true.

quizData.question: If `val a = true` and `val b = false`, what is the result of `a && b`?
quizData.explanation: Because they were not BOTH true, the overall AND check fails (returns false).

slides.title: The OR Operator (||) 🔀
slides.content:
The `||` symbol (two vertical pipes) means OR. 

If the left side OR the right side is true, the entire statement is true. It only takes one!

quizData.question: If `val c = false` and `val d = true`, what is the result of `c || d`?
quizData.explanation: Because at least ONE of them represents true (d), the overall OR check succeeds.

slides.title: The NOT Operator (!) 🔄
slides.content:
The `!` symbol means NOT. It simply flips a boolean value to the exact opposite.

`!true` becomes `false`.
`!false` becomes `true`.
